scope_boundary: repository living-book mechanics only; no claim about ASI system behavior, manuscript finality, or reader-release quality is promoted
transition_reason: The repository demonstrates manifest-driven Quarto structure, source queues, claim/evidence matrices, proof manifests, validation scripts, changelogs, release records, and reader-generation mechanics. The chapter claim remains broader than those mechanics because full evidence-release review, proof adequacy review, human reader review, and artifact release records are not complete.
required_artifacts: reviewed reader manuscript record if reader quality is claimed
first executable behavior tests beyond schema shape validation
negative_results: reader manuscript is generated and spot-reviewed only
no v1.0 evidence release record exists
no behavior-test harness has promoted a claim
downgrade_triggers: generated reader artifact claimed without review
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after a bounded claim is rewritten or scoped to the repository mechanics already validated, then accepted through review with release-record evidence.
limitations: local validation does not prove manuscript quality
render success does not prove release readiness
generated reader source is not a reviewed reader manuscript
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent external audit
acceptance_blockers: proof adequacy and test harness work remain open
non_claims: does not claim the book is final
does not claim reader artifacts exist
does not prove any ASI runtime behavior
